Description.Name: Albatross
Description.Details: The Albatross was originally designed by the Free Worlds League in 3053 with an eye toward a possible future conflict with the then advancing Clans. The fruit of 'Project Omega,' a Free Worlds League Military project in 3052 to create an assault 'Mech capable of facing Clan OmniMechs based off scant reports of Clan fighting tactics and weapons and refit kits requested by the Federated Commonwealth and Draconis Combine, the first Albatross was delivered after only five months of design and testing with mass production being authorized by the League High Command a mere ten months after it was commissioned. Built using a Hermes 380 XL engine to minimize weight and maximize free tonnage for its weapon loadout, the Albatross also uses a variety of weapons in an attempt to make the 'Mech effective at any range. The 'Mech has average mobility with a maximum speed of 64.8 km/h and also makes use of double heat sinks for their greater heat dissipation capability. The Albatross carries thirteen tons of Starslab/3 armor. Finally, the 'Mech is also equipped with CASE to protect against ammunition explosions. The Albatross does not specialize in one role and is more of a workhorse design than anything else. The 4U variant of the Albatross is a basic upgrade of the design. The 'Mech replaces the medium lasers with a pair of ER medium lasers, the LB 10-X Autocannon with a Light Gauss Rifle, the SRM-6 with a Streak SRM-6 launcher. The ER large laser is removed and replaced by an ER small laser, while the armor is increased by more than three tons and upgraded to ferro-fibrous.

YangsThoughts: The only reason this thing ever got a bad rap was that some of the later versions used a composite internal structure which made it fragile if the armor was breached. The earlier versions are pefectly solid mechs, though they're a bit too generalist to really reach maximum effectiveness. This rework definitely improves the overall firepower while also improving the armor, but if you have a clanspec gauss then it'll be a better upgrade.
